---
title: "Library installation"

---

This notebook is for making ArchR and Giotto libraries at separate paths for R 4.4.0 

# Creating ArchR library

First load these modules into VS code or interactive session - 
module load gdal/3.8.4
module load geos/3.12.1
module load proj/9.3.1
module load sqlite3/3.44.2
module load openblas/0.3.23 
module load gcc/12.2.0
module load glpk/5.0

module load R 
R


```{r}
.libPaths('/projectnb/paxlab/presh/ArchR_libs')
.libPaths()

if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools")
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
devtools::install_github("GreenleafLab/ArchR", ref="master", repos = BiocManager::repositories())
```

```{r}
library(ArchR)

# RcppArmadillo gave me issues, use this to install!
install.packages("RcppArmadillo", type = "source",
  configure.args = c(
    "--with-lapack",
    "--with-blas"
  ))
```


# Creating Giotto library

```{r}
.libPaths('/projectnb/paxlab/presh/giotto_libs')
.libPaths()

devtools::install_github("drieslab/GiottoUtils@R4.4.0", force = T)
remotes::install_github("rspatial/terra")
packageVersion('terra')
devtools::install_version("Matrix", version = "1.6-5")
devtools::install_github("drieslab/GiottoClass@R4.4.0",  force = T)
devtools::install_github("drieslab/GiottoVisuals@R4.4.0", force = T)
devtools::install_github("drieslab/Giotto@R4.4.0",  force = T)

library(Giotto)

# Ensure Giotto can access the python env
set_giotto_python_path(python_path = '/usr3/graduate/preshita/.conda/envs/giotto_env/bin/python', verbose = NULL, initialize = TRUE)

```
